title: Mantle Moulding Headboard
summary: A queen headboard with a plain 2x6 frame dressed up entirely with applied moulding — the frame itself is simple; the moulding pattern is where the style choices happen.
description:
This headboard's structure is genuinely simple — a basic 2x6 frame, cleats down the sides, and a 1/4" plywood back panel — and every bit of its character comes from the moulding applied on top of that plain frame afterward, not from anything complicated in the joinery. Build the frame first sized for a queen mattress, widening it for a king or narrowing it for a twin or full by adjusting the frame width alone; every other dimension stays the same.

Cleats down the sides support the front boards, and the header cap goes on across the top once the front face is built up and flush. The back panel fills the frame's open face last, before any moulding goes on — **you could substitute an upholstered panel here instead of plywood** if you want a padded look rather than a flat painted one.

The moulding is genuinely flexible: this plan calls for three 7-foot lengths of moulding trim in the sizes noted, applied directly over the finished frame front, with an optional second moulding pattern on the center panel for extra detail. There's real room to choose a plainer or more ornate moulding profile here to match your own taste — the frame underneath doesn't change either way.

materials.name: Moulding trim for center panel, 8 ft lengths, optional
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Build the frame
steps.body: Build the basic 2x6 frame sized for a queen mattress, using pocket holes and 2-1/2" pocket hole screws. Widen for a king or narrow for a twin/full by adjusting frame width only.

steps.title: Attach cleats to the sides
steps.body: Attach the side cleats to the inside faces of the frame sides to support the front boards.

steps.title: Build up the front face
steps.body: Attach additional boards to the front, taking time to make sure the ends match up perfectly and everything sits flush.

steps.title: Finish the end with blocking
steps.body: Add the end blocking pieces to finish off the frame ends.

steps.title: Add the header
steps.body: Attach the header cleat, then the header cap across the top with screws.

steps.title: Fill in the back panel
steps.body: Attach the 1/4" plywood back panel to fill in the open frame face, or substitute an upholstered panel for a padded look.

steps.title: Add the moulding
steps.body: Apply the moulding trim directly over the finished frame front in your chosen pattern, mitering corners as needed. Optionally add a second moulding pattern on the center panel for extra detail.
materials: Moulding trim for center panel, 8 ft lengths, optional

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

images.alt: Queen headboard with applied mantle moulding
